1→# Sprint 5: Simulation & Intelligence 2→ 3→**Duration:** 2 weeks 4→**Sprint Goal:** Implement AI-assisted design and headless simulation capabilities for testing and validation 5→**Success Criteria:** AI command palette functional and time-travel simulator provides accurate experiment preview 6→ 7→--- 8→ 9→## Sprint Objectives 10→ 11→1. **Headless Runtime**: Package ForgeScript runtime for WebAssembly execution and simulation 12→2. **AI Command Palette**: Natural language experiment design assistance 13→3. **Time-Travel Simulator**: Interactive experiment preview with scrubbing timeline 14→4. **Virtual Device Lab**: Simulation environment for testing without hardware 15→5. **Performance Optimization**: Memory budget enforcement and optimization 16→ 17→--- 18→ 19→## Sprint Backlog 20→ 21→### Epic: Headless Simulation Engine 22→**Story Points: 9** 23→ 24→#### SIM-001: Package @psyforge/headless (WASM) & Memory Budget Test 25→**Points: 6 | Priority: Critical** 26→ 27→**Description:** Create a WebAssembly-compiled version of the ForgeScript runtime for high-performance simulation and testing. 28→ 29→**Technical Requirements:** 30→- Compile ForgeScript runtime to WebAssembly for deterministic execution 31→- Implement virtual time progression for accelerated simulation 32→- Memory management with strict budget enforcement (<100MB target) 33→- Statistical models for generating realistic participant responses 34→ 35→**Acceptance Criteria:** 36→- [ ] WASM runtime executes ForgeScript experiments deterministically 37→- [ ] Virtual time allows simulation at 100x+ real-time speed 38→- [ ] Memory usage stays under 100MB for standard experiments (200+ trials) 39→- [ ] Response generation uses configurable statistical models 40→- [ ] Headless execution produces identical results across runs 41→ 42→**WASM Implementation:** 43→```typescript 44→// @psyforge/headless 45→export interface HeadlessConfig { 46→ experiment: CompiledExperiment; 47→ cognitiveModel: CognitiveModel; 48→ participants: number; 49→ speedMultiplier: number; // 1 = real-time, 100 = 100x speed 50→ randomSeed?: number; // For reproducible simulation 51→} 52→ 53→export class HeadlessRunner { 54→ constructor(config: HeadlessConfig); 55→ 56→ // Run complete experiment simulation 57→ async simulate(): Promise; 58→ 59→ // Run until specific time point 60→ async simulateUntil(virtualTime: number): Promise; 61→ 62→ // Generate participant response based on cognitive model 63→ generateResponse(stimulus: Stimulus, context: TrialContext): Response; 64→ 65→ // Memory usage monitoring 66→ getMemoryUsage(): MemoryMetrics; 67→} 68→ 69→// Cognitive models for realistic response generation 70→export interface CognitiveModel { 71→ // Reaction time distribution (e.g., Ex-Gaussian) 72→ reactionTime(stimulus: Stimulus, difficulty: number): number; 73→ 74→ // Accuracy model based on stimulus properties 75→ accuracy(stimulus: Stimulus, fatigue: number): boolean; 76→ 77→ // Learning effects over trials 78→ learningCurve(trialNumber: number, condition: string): number; 79→} 80→ 81→// Built-in cognitive models 82→export const CognitiveModels = { 83→ // Simple RT model with practice effects 84→ simpleRT: { 85→ baseRT: 400, // ms 86→ practiceEffect: 0.02, // 2% improvement per trial 87→ fatigueEffect: 0.001, // 0.1% slowing per trial 88→ errorRate: 0.05 // 5% base error rate 89→ }, 90→ 91→ // Stroop task model with interference effects 92→ stroopModel: { 93→ congruentRT: 350, 94→ incongruentRT: 450, 95→ interferenceDecay: 0.1, // Learning reduces interference 96→ speedAccuracyTradeoff: 0.3 97→ } 98→}; 99→``` 100→ 101→#### SIM-002: Virtual Device Integration 102→**Points: 3 | Priority: High** 103→ 104→**Description:** Integrate virtual devices for simulation of hardware-dependent experiments. 105→ 106→**Technical Requirements:** 107→- Virtual keyboard/mouse with configurable response characteristics 108→- Virtual eye-tracker with gaze pattern simulation 109→- Virtual EEG with synthetic signal generation 110→- Configurable device latency and precision models 111→ 112→**Acceptance Criteria:** 113→- [ ] Virtual devices produce realistic response patterns 114→- [ ] Device latency simulation matches real hardware characteristics 115→- [ ] EEG simulation generates plausible signals for ERP analysis 116→- [ ] Eye-tracking simulation follows realistic saccade patterns 117→- [ ] Hardware-dependent experiments run in simulation 118→ 119→--- 120→ 121→### Epic: AI-Assisted Design 122→**Story Points: 7** 123→ 124→#### AI-001: AI Command Palette Backend 125→**Points: 4 | Priority: High** 126→ 127→**Description:** Implement natural language processing for experiment design assistance. 128→ 129→**Technical Requirements:** 130→- LLM integration for parsing design requests 131→- ForgeScript code generation from natural language 132→- Validation of AI-generated code against schemas 133→- Context-aware suggestions based on current experiment 134→ 135→**Acceptance Criteria:** 136→- [ ] Natural language commands generate valid ForgeScript code 137→- [ ] AI suggestions contextually appropriate for current experiment 138→- [ ] Generated code passes all validation checks 139→- [ ] Command palette provides helpful suggestions and examples 140→- [ ] Error handling for ambiguous or impossible requests 141→ 142→**AI Implementation:** 143→```typescript 144→// @psyforge/ai-assistant 145→export interface DesignCommand { 146→ intent: 'add_stimulus' | 'add_trial' | 'modify_timing' | 'add_condition'; 147→ parameters: Record; 148→ context: ExperimentContext; 149→} 150→ 151→export class AIDesignAssistant { 152→ constructor(apiKey: string, model: 'gpt-4' | 'claude-3'); 153→ 154→ // Parse natural language into structured command 155→ async parseCommand(input: string, context: ExperimentContext): Promise; 156→ 157→ // Generate ForgeScript code for command 158→ async generateCode(command: DesignCommand): Promise; 159→ 160→ // Validate generated code 161→ async validateCode(code: string): Promise; 162→ 163→ // Suggest improvements or alternatives 164→ async suggestImprovements(experiment: CompiledExperiment): Promise; 165→} 166→ 167→// Example usage 168→const assistant = new AIDesignAssistant(process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY, 'gpt-4'); 169→ 170→// "Add 20 Stroop trials with equal numbers of congruent and incongruent" 171→const command = await assistant.parseCommand(userInput, currentExperiment); 172→const code = await assistant.generateCode(command); 173→const validation = await assistant.validateCode(code); 174→ 175→if (validation.valid) { 176→ applyCodeToExperiment(code); 177→} else { 178→ showValidationErrors(validation.errors); 179→} 180→``` 181→ 182→#### GUI-010: AI Command Palette UI Integration 183→**Points: 3 | Priority: High** 184→ 185→**Description:** Build the user interface for AI-assisted experiment design. 186→ 187→**Technical Requirements:** 188→- Command palette with natural language input 189→- Real-time suggestions and autocompletion 190→- Preview of generated code before application 191→- History of AI commands and ability to undo 192→ 193→**Acceptance Criteria:** 194→- [ ] Command palette accessible via keyboard shortcut (Cmd/Ctrl+K) 195→- [ ] Real-time suggestions appear as user types 196→- [ ] Generated code preview shown before application 197→- [ ] Command history allows easy repetition of common actions 198→- [ ] Undo/redo works correctly with AI-generated changes 199→ 200→**UI Components:** 201→```svelte 202→ 203→ 234→ 235→

1→# Sprint 10: Alpha Release
     2→
     3→**Duration:** 2 weeks  
     4→**Sprint Goal:** Deploy alpha version and onboard founding research labs for real-world validation  
     5→**Success Criteria:** 3 founding labs running production studies with comprehensive support and monitoring
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Sprint Objectives
    10→
    11→1. **Alpha Release Candidate**: Cut production-ready alpha release with complete feature set
    12→2. **Production Deployment**: Deploy to production infrastructure with monitoring and scaling
    13→3. **Founding Lab Onboarding**: Successfully onboard 3 research labs with hands-on support
    14→4. **Support Infrastructure**: Establish customer success and technical support systems
    15→5. **Validation Studies**: Begin data collection for academic publication and validation
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## Sprint Backlog
    20→
    21→### Epic: Release Engineering
    22→**Story Points: 6**
    23→
    24→#### REL-001: Cut Alpha Release Candidate
    25→**Points: 3 | Priority: Critical**
    26→
    27→**Description:** Create production-ready alpha release with complete feature set and quality validation.
    28→
    29→**Technical Requirements:**
    30→- Comprehensive testing across all major workflows
    31→- Performance validation under realistic loads
    32→- Security audit and penetration testing
    33→- Release artifact generation with proper versioning
    34→- Rollback procedures and emergency patches
    35→
    36→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    37→- [ ] All integration tests pass across multiple environments
    38→- [ ] Performance benchmarks meet specified targets
    39→- [ ] Security audit reveals no critical vulnerabilities
    40→- [ ] Release artifacts signed and versioned appropriately
    41→- [ ] Rollback procedures tested and documented
    42→- [ ] Emergency patch process established
    43→
    44→**Release Process:**
    45→```yaml
    46→# .github/workflows/alpha-release.yml
    47→name: Alpha Release
    48→
    49→on:
    50→  push:
    51→    tags:
    52→      - 'v*-alpha.*'
    53→
    54→jobs:
    55→  comprehensive-testing:
    56→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    57→    strategy:
    58→      matrix:
    59→        browser: [chrome, firefox, safari]
    60→        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]
    61→    
    62→    steps:
    63→      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    64→      
    65→      - name: Setup Node.js
    66→        uses: actions/setup-node@v3
    67→        with:
    68→          node-version: '18'
    69→          cache: 'npm'
    70→      
    71→      - name: Install dependencies
    72→        run: npm ci
    73→      
    74→      - name: Run unit tests
    75→        run: npm run test:unit
    76→      
    77→      - name: Run integration tests
    78→        run: npm run test:integration
    79→      
    80→      - name: Run E2E tests
    81→        run: npm run test:e2e --browser=${{ matrix.browser }}
    82→      
    83→      - name: Performance benchmarks
    84→        run: npm run test:performance
    85→      
    86→      - name: Security audit
    87→        run: npm audit --audit-level=moderate
    88→  
    89→  build-artifacts:
    90→    needs: comprehensive-testing
    91→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    92→    
    93→    steps:
    94→      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    95→      
    96→      - name: Build production artifacts
    97→        run: |
    98→          npm run build:studio
    99→          npm run build:runtime
   100→          npm run build:headless
   101→      
   102→      - name: Generate checksums
   103→        run: |
   104→          find dist/ -type f -exec sha256sum {} \; > checksums.txt
   105→      
   106→      - name: Sign artifacts
   107→        run: |
   108→          gpg --batch --yes --detach-sign --armor checksums.txt
   109→      
   110→      - name: Upload artifacts
   111→        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
   112→        with:
   113→          name: alpha-release-${{ github.ref_name }}
   114→          path: |
   115→            dist/
   116→            checksums.txt
   117→            checksums.txt.asc
   118→  
   119→  deploy-staging:
   120→    needs: build-artifacts
   121→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   122→    environment: staging
   123→    
   124→    steps:
   125→      - name: Deploy to staging
   126→        run: |
   127→          # Deploy to staging environment
   128→          # Run smoke tests
   129→          # Validate deployment
   130→  
   131→  security-audit:
   132→    needs: deploy-staging
   133→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   134→    
   135→    steps:
   136→      - name: Run security scan
   137→        run: |
   138→          # OWASP ZAP scan
   139→          # Dependency vulnerability check
   140→          # Infrastructure security audit
   141→  
   142→  create-release:
   143→    needs: [comprehensive-testing, build-artifacts, security-audit]
   144→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   145→    
   146→    steps:
   147→      - name: Create GitHub Release
   148→        uses: actions/create-release@v1
   149→        env:
   150→          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
   151→        with:
   152→          tag_name: ${{ github.ref }}
   153→          release_name: Psycho-Forge Alpha ${{ github.ref }}
   154→          body: |
   155→            # Psycho-Forge Alpha Release
   156→            
   157→            ## 🎯 Alpha Release Highlights
   158→            - ⚡ **≤2ms stimulus timing precision** validated across platforms
   159→            - 🔄 **Real-time collaboration** with CRDT synchronization  
   160→            - 🤖 **AI-assisted design** with natural language commands
   161→            - 📊 **Analysis-ready export** with generated R/Python scripts
   162→            - 🏛️ **Academic integration** with automatic DOI assignment
   163→            
   164→            ## 🧪 Validated Features
   165→            - Complete experiment lifecycle from design to publication
   166→            - Mirror-Canvas visual programming with code synchronization
   167→            - Universal Recruitment Fabric with Prolific integration
   168→            - Device Abstraction Layer with virtual testing environment
   169→            - Paradigm Hub with 10 classic experimental paradigms
   170→            
   171→            ## ⚠️ Alpha Release Notes
   172→            This is an alpha release intended for **founding research labs** only.
   173→            Features are complete but may have edge cases. Production use should
   174→            include comprehensive testing of your specific workflows.
   175→            
   176→            ## 📋 System Requirements
   177→            - **Browser**: Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+
   178→            - **Desktop**: Optional Tauri app for maximum timing precision
   179→            - **Memory**: 4GB RAM minimum, 8GB recommended
   180→            - **Network**: Stable internet for collaboration features
   181→            
   182→            ## 🚀 Getting Started
   183→            1. Visit [studio.psyforge.org](https://studio.psyforge.org)
   184→            2. Complete the "First-Run Orbit" onboarding
   185→            3. Try the demo Stroop task
   186→            4. Join our [founding labs Slack](https://psyforge-alpha.slack.com)
   187→            
   188→            ## 📞 Support
   189→            - 📧 Email: alpha-support@psyforge.org  
   190→            - 💬 Slack: #alpha-support channel
   191→            - 📚 Docs: [docs.psyforge.org](https://docs.psyforge.org)
   192→            - 🐛 Issues: [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/psyforge/platform/issues)
   193→          draft: false
   194→          prerelease: true
   195→```
   196→
   197→#### INF-004: Deploy Alpha to Production
   198→**Points: 3 | Priority: Critical**
   199→
   200→**Description:** Deploy alpha release to production infrastructure with monitoring, scaling, and observability.
   201→
   202→**Technical Requirements:**
   203→- Kubernetes deployment with auto-scaling capabilities

   215→- [ ] Health checks and alerting operational
   216→
   217→**Production Infrastructure:**
   218→```yaml
   219→# k8s/production/studio-deployment.yml
   220→apiVersion: apps/v1

   222→metadata:

   224→  namespace: production
   225→spec:
   226→  replicas: 3
   227→  selector:
   228→    matchLabels:
   229→      app: psycho-forge-studio
   230→  template:
   231→    metadata:
   232→      labels:
   233→        app: psycho-forge-studio
   234→    spec:
   235→      containers:
   236→      - name: studio
   237→        image: psyforge/studio:v1.0.0-alpha.1
   238→        ports:
   239→        - containerPort: 3000
   240→        env:
   241→        - name: NODE_ENV
   242→          value: "production"
   243→        - name: DATABASE_URL
   244→          valueFrom:
   245→            secretKeyRef:
   246→              name: db-credentials
   247→              key: url
   248→        resources:
   249→          requests:
   250→            memory: "512Mi"
   251→            cpu: "250m"
   252→          limits:
   253→            memory: "1Gi" 
   254→            cpu: "500m"
   255→        livenessProbe:
   256→          httpGet:
   257→            path: /health
   258→            port: 3000
   259→          initialDelaySeconds: 30
   260→          periodSeconds: 10
   261→        readinessProbe:

   312→### Epic: Founding Lab Onboarding
   313→**Story Points: 8**
   314→
   315→#### CSM-001: Onboard 3 Founding Labs
   316→**Points: 5 | Priority: Critical**
   317→
   318→**Description:** Successfully onboard and support 3 founding research labs to validate platform with real studies.
   319→
   320→**Technical Requirements:**

   334→
   335→**Founding Lab Onboarding Process:**
   336→```typescript
   337→// Customer Success Management System
   338→export class FoundingLabOnboarding {
   339→  private labs: FoundingLab[];
   340→  private onboardingSteps: OnboardingStep[];
   341→  private supportTickets: SupportTicket[];
   342→  
   343→  constructor() {
   344→    this.labs = [];
   345→    this.onboardingSteps = this.defineOnboardingProcess();
   346→    this.supportTickets = [];
   347→  }
   348→  
   349→  async onboardLab(labInfo: LabInfo): Promise<OnboardingPlan> {
   350→    // Create custom onboarding plan based on lab needs
   351→    const lab = new FoundingLab(labInfo);
   352→    
   353→    // Assess lab's specific requirements
   354→    const requirements = await this.assessLabRequirements(lab);
   355→    
   356→    // Create custom onboarding plan
   357→    const plan = this.createCustomOnboardingPlan(lab, requirements);
   358→    
   359→    // Schedule initial kickoff meeting
   360→    await this.scheduleKickoffMeeting(lab, plan);
   361→    
   362→    // Set up dedicated support channel
   363→    await this.createDedicatedSupportChannel(lab);
   364→    
   365→    this.labs.push(lab);
   366→    return plan;
   367→  }
   368→  
   369→  private async assessLabRequirements(lab: FoundingLab): Promise<LabRequirements> {
   370→    return {
   371→      primaryResearchArea: lab.researchArea,
   372→      typicalExperimentTypes: lab.experimentTypes,
   373→      participantVolume: lab.expectedParticipants,
   374→      existingTools: lab.currentTools,
   375→      technicalExpertise: lab.teamTechnicalLevel,
   376→      integrationNeeds: lab.requiredIntegrations,
   377→      timelineConstraints: lab.projectTimelines,
   378→      collaborationNeeds: lab.collaborationRequirements
   379→    };
   380→  }
   381→  
   382→  private createCustomOnboardingPlan(lab: FoundingLab, requirements: LabRequirements): OnboardingPlan {
   383→    const basePlan = this.getBaseOnboardingPlan();
   384→    
   385→    // Customize based on lab requirements
   386→    if (requirements.technicalExpertise === 'low') {
   387→      basePlan.addExtraTrainingSession('technical-foundations');
   388→      basePlan.increaseSupporLevel('white-glove');
   389→    }
   390→    
   391→    if (requirements.integrationNeeds.includes('eeg')) {
   392→      basePlan.addSpecializedSession('eeg-integration');
   393→      basePlan.assignSpecialist('neurophysiology');
   394→    }
   395→    
   396→    if (requirements.participantVolume > 1000) {
   397→      basePlan.addSession('high-volume-recruitment');
   398→      basePlan.setPriority('enterprise');
   399→    }
   400→    
   401→    return basePlan;
   402→  }
   403→  
   404→  // Track onboarding progress
   405→  async trackOnboardingProgress(labId: string): Promise<OnboardingProgress> {
   406→    const lab = this.labs.find(l => l.id === labId);
   407→    if (!lab) throw new Error('Lab not found');
   408→    
   409→    const completedSteps = lab.onboardingSteps.filter(step => step.completed);
   410→    const totalSteps = lab.onboardingSteps.length;
   411→    
   412→    return {
   413→      labId: labId,
   414→      progress: (completedSteps.length / totalSteps) * 100,
   415→      currentStep: lab.getCurrentStep(),
   416→      estimatedCompletion: lab.getEstimatedCompletion(),
   417→      blockers: lab.getBlockers(),
   418→      nextActions: lab.getNextActions()
   419→    };
   420→  }
   421→}
   422→
   423→// Target Founding Labs (examples)
   424→const targetFoundingLabs = [
   425→  {
   426→    name: 'Stanford Psychology Dept',
   427→    contact: 'Dr. Jane Smith',
   428→    researchArea: 'Cognitive Psychology',
   429→    experimentTypes: ['attention', 'memory', 'decision-making'],
   430→    expectedParticipants: 2000,
   431→    timeline: 'Q4 2025 studies'
   432→  },
   433→  
   434→  {
   435→    name: 'MIT Brain & Cognitive Sciences',
   436→    contact: 'Dr. John Johnson', 
   437→    researchArea: 'Computational Neuroscience',
   438→    experimentTypes: ['eeg', 'behavioral', 'modeling'],
   439→    expectedParticipants: 500,
   440→    timeline: 'Q1 2026 grant deadline'
   441→  },
   442→  
   443→  {
   444→    name: 'UC Berkeley Psychology',
   445→    contact: 'Dr. Sarah Williams',
   446→    researchArea: 'Social Psychology', 
   447→    experimentTypes: ['online-studies', 'cross-cultural'],
   448→    expectedParticipants: 5000,
   449→    timeline: 'Ongoing studies'
   450→  }
   451→];
   452→```

   540→#### SUP-002: Monitoring & Alerting Systems

# Sprint 10: Alpha Release

**Duration:** 2 weeks  
**Sprint Goal:** Deploy alpha version and onboard founding research labs for real-world validation  
**Success Criteria:** 3 founding labs running production studies with comprehensive support and monitoring

---

## Sprint Objectives

1. **Alpha Release Candidate**: Cut production-ready alpha release with complete feature set
2. **Production Deployment**: Deploy to production infrastructure with monitoring and scaling
3. **Founding Lab Onboarding**: Successfully onboard 3 research labs with hands-on support
4. **Support Infrastructure**: Establish customer success and technical support systems
5. **Validation Studies**: Begin data collection for academic publication and validation

---

## Sprint Backlog

### Epic: Release Engineering
**Story Points: 6**

#### REL-001: Cut Alpha Release Candidate
**Points: 3 | Priority: Critical**

**Description:** Create production-ready alpha release with complete feature set and quality validation.

**Technical Requirements:**
- Comprehensive testing across all major workflows
- Performance validation under realistic loads
- Security audit and penetration testing
- Release artifact generation with proper versioning
- Rollback procedures and emergency patches

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] All integration tests pass across multiple environments
- [ ] Performance benchmarks meet specified targets
- [ ] Security audit reveals no critical vulnerabilities
- [ ] Release artifacts signed and versioned appropriately
- [ ] Rollback procedures tested and documented
- [ ] Emergency patch process established

**Release Process:**
```yaml
# .github/workflows/alpha-release.yml
name: Alpha Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*-alpha.*'

jobs:
  comprehensive-testing:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        browser: [chrome, firefox, safari]
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v3
        with:
          node-version: '18'
          cache: 'npm'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
      
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm run test:unit
      
      - name: Run integration tests
        run: npm run test:integration
      
      - name: Run E2E tests
        run: npm run test:e2e --browser=${{ matrix.browser }}
      
      - name: Performance benchmarks
        run: npm run test:performance
      
      - name: Security audit
        run: npm audit --audit-level=moderate
  
  build-artifacts:
    needs: comprehensive-testing
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Build production artifacts
        run: |
          npm run build:studio
          npm run build:runtime
          npm run build:headless
      
      - name: Generate checksums
        run: |
          find dist/ -type f -exec sha256sum {} \; > checksums.txt
      
      - name: Sign artifacts
        run: |
          gpg --batch --yes --detach-sign --armor checksums.txt
      
      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
        with:
          name: alpha-release-${{ github.ref_name }}
          path: |
            dist/
            checksums.txt
            checksums.txt.asc
  
  deploy-staging:
    needs: build-artifacts
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging
    
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to staging
        run: |
          # Deploy to staging environment
          # Run smoke tests
          # Validate deployment
  
  security-audit:
    needs: deploy-staging
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Run security scan
        run: |
          # OWASP ZAP scan
          # Dependency vulnerability check
          # Infrastructure security audit
  
  create-release:
    needs: [comprehensive-testing, build-artifacts, security-audit]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: actions/create-release@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ github.ref }}
          release_name: Psycho-Forge Alpha ${{ github.ref }}
          body: |
            # Psycho-Forge Alpha Release
            
            ## 🎯 Alpha Release Highlights
            - ⚡ **≤2ms stimulus timing precision** validated across platforms
            - 🔄 **Real-time collaboration** with CRDT synchronization  
            - 🤖 **AI-assisted design** with natural language commands
            - 📊 **Analysis-ready export** with generated R/Python scripts
            - 🏛️ **Academic integration** with automatic DOI assignment
            
            ## 🧪 Validated Features
            - Complete experiment lifecycle from design to publication
            - Mirror-Canvas visual programming with code synchronization
            - Universal Recruitment Fabric with Prolific integration
            - Device Abstraction Layer with virtual testing environment
            - Paradigm Hub with 10 classic experimental paradigms
            
            ## ⚠️ Alpha Release Notes
            This is an alpha release intended for **founding research labs** only.
            Features are complete but may have edge cases. Production use should
            include comprehensive testing of your specific workflows.
            
            ## 📋 System Requirements
            - **Browser**: Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+
            - **Desktop**: Optional Tauri app for maximum timing precision
            - **Memory**: 4GB RAM minimum, 8GB recommended
            - **Network**: Stable internet for collaboration features
            
            ## 🚀 Getting Started
            1. Visit [studio.psyforge.org](https://studio.psyforge.org)
            2. Complete the "First-Run Orbit" onboarding
            3. Try the demo Stroop task
            4. Join our [founding labs Slack](https://psyforge-alpha.slack.com)
            
            ## 📞 Support
            - 📧 Email: alpha-support@psyforge.org  
            - 💬 Slack: #alpha-support channel
            - 📚 Docs: [docs.psyforge.org](https://docs.psyforge.org)
            - 🐛 Issues: [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/psyforge/platform/issues)
          draft: false
          prerelease: true
```

#### INF-004: Deploy Alpha to Production
**Points: 3 | Priority: Critical**

**Founding Lab Onboarding Process:**
```typescript
// Customer Success Management System
export class FoundingLabOnboarding {
  private labs: FoundingLab[];
  private onboardingSteps: OnboardingStep[];
  private supportTickets: SupportTicket[];
  
  constructor() {
    this.labs = [];
    this.onboardingSteps = this.defineOnboardingProcess();
    this.supportTickets = [];
  }
  
  async onboardLab(labInfo: LabInfo): Promise<OnboardingPlan> {
    // Create custom onboarding plan based on lab needs
    const lab = new FoundingLab(labInfo);
    
    // Assess lab's specific requirements
    const requirements = await this.assessLabRequirements(lab);
    
    // Create custom onboarding plan
    const plan = this.createCustomOnboardingPlan(lab, requirements);
    
    // Schedule initial kickoff meeting
    await this.scheduleKickoffMeeting(lab, plan);
    
    // Set up dedicated support channel
    await this.createDedicatedSupportChannel(lab);
    
    this.labs.push(lab);
    return plan;
  }
  
  private async assessLabRequirements(lab: FoundingLab): Promise<LabRequirements> {
    return {
      primaryResearchArea: lab.researchArea,
      typicalExperimentTypes: lab.experimentTypes,
      participantVolume: lab.expectedParticipants,
      existingTools: lab.currentTools,
      technicalExpertise: lab.teamTechnicalLevel,
      integrationNeeds: lab.requiredIntegrations,
      timelineConstraints: lab.projectTimelines,
      collaborationNeeds: lab.collaborationRequirements
    };
  }
  
  private createCustomOnboardingPlan(lab: FoundingLab, requirements: LabRequirements): OnboardingPlan {
    const basePlan = this.getBaseOnboardingPlan();
    
    // Customize based on lab requirements
    if (requirements.technicalExpertise === 'low') {
      basePlan.addExtraTrainingSession('technical-foundations');
      basePlan.increaseSupporLevel('white-glove');
    }
    
    if (requirements.integrationNeeds.includes('eeg')) {
      basePlan.addSpecializedSession('eeg-integration');
      basePlan.assignSpecialist('neurophysiology');
    }
    
    if (requirements.participantVolume > 1000) {
      basePlan.addSession('high-volume-recruitment');
      basePlan.setPriority('enterprise');
    }
    
    return basePlan;
  }
  
  // Track onboarding progress
  async trackOnboardingProgress(labId: string): Promise<OnboardingProgress> {
    const lab = this.labs.find(l => l.id === labId);
    if (!lab) throw new Error('Lab not found');
    
    const completedSteps = lab.onboardingSteps.filter(step => step.completed);
    const totalSteps = lab.onboardingSteps.length;
    
    return {
      labId: labId,
      progress: (completedSteps.length / totalSteps) * 100,
      currentStep: lab.getCurrentStep(),
      estimatedCompletion: lab.getEstimatedCompletion(),
      blockers: lab.getBlockers(),
      nextActions: lab.getNextActions()
    };
  }
}

// Target Founding Labs (examples)

  {
    name: 'Stanford Psychology Dept',

    researchArea: 'Cognitive Psychology',
    experimentTypes: ['attention', 'memory', 'decision-making'],
    expectedParticipants: 2000,
    timeline: 'Q4 2025 studies'
  },
  
  {
    name: 'MIT Brain & Cognitive Sciences',
    contact: 'Dr. John Johnson', 
    researchArea: 'Computational Neuroscience',
    experimentTypes: ['eeg', 'behavioral', 'modeling'],
    expectedParticipants: 500,
    timeline: 'Q1 2026 grant deadline'
  },

    expectedParticipants: 5000,

  }
];

    24→#### ARE-001: Analysis-Ready Export (Tidy CSV)

    27→**Description:** Implement comprehensive data export system that produces analysis-ready datasets in multiple formats.
    28→

    44→**Data Export Implementation:**
    45→```typescript
    46→// @psyforge/export
    47→export class AnalysisReadyExporter {
    48→  private experiment: CompiledExperiment;
    49→  private rawData: ExperimentData[];
    50→  private metadata: ExperimentMetadata;
    51→  
    52→  constructor(experiment: CompiledExperiment, data: ExperimentData[], metadata: ExperimentMetadata) {
    53→    this.experiment = experiment;
    54→    this.rawData = data;
    55→    this.metadata = metadata;
    56→  }
    57→  
    58→  // Export to tidy CSV format
    59→  async exportCSV(options: CSVExportOptions = {}): Promise<CSVExportResult> {
    60→    const tidyData = this.transformToTidyFormat(this.rawData);
    61→    const validated = this.validateData(tidyData);
    62→    
    63→    const csv = this.generateCSV(validated, {
    64→      includeHeaders: options.includeHeaders ?? true,
    65→      delimiter: options.delimiter ?? ',',
    66→      encoding: options.encoding ?? 'utf-8'
    67→    });
    68→    
    69→    const metadata = this.generateDataDictionary(validated);
    70→    
    71→    return {
    72→      data: csv,
    73→      metadata: metadata,
    74→      fileName: this.generateFileName('csv'),
    75→      schema: this.generateJSONSchema(validated)
    76→    };
    77→  }
    78→  
    79→  // Export to Parquet for performance
    80→  async exportParquet(): Promise<ParquetExportResult> {
    81→    const tidyData = this.transformToTidyFormat(this.rawData);
    82→    const validated = this.validateData(tidyData);
    83→    
    84→    // Convert to Arrow Table for Parquet serialization
    85→    const table = this.convertToArrowTable(validated);
    86→    const parquetBuffer = await this.serializeToParquet(table);
    87→    
    88→    return {
    89→      data: parquetBuffer,
    90→      fileName: this.generateFileName('parquet'),
    91→      schema: table.schema,
    92→      compressionRatio: this.calculateCompressionRatio(validated, parquetBuffer)
    93→    };
    94→  }
    95→  
    96→  // Transform raw experiment data to tidy format
    97→  private transformToTidyFormat(rawData: ExperimentData[]): TidyDataRow[] {
    98→    return rawData.map((trial, index) => ({
    99→      // Participant information
   100→      participant_id: trial.participantId,
   101→      session_id: trial.sessionId,
   102→      age: trial.demographics?.age,
   103→      gender: trial.demographics?.gender,
   104→      
   105→      // Trial identifiers
   106→      trial_number: index + 1,
   107→      trial_id: trial.trialId,
   108→      trial_type: trial.trialType,
   109→      block_id: trial.blockId,
   110→      timeline_id: trial.timelineId,
   111→      
   112→      // Experimental conditions
   113→      condition: trial.condition,
   114→      condition_level_1: trial.conditionLevels?.[0],
   115→      condition_level_2: trial.conditionLevels?.[1], 
   116→      condition_level_3: trial.conditionLevels?.[2],
   117→      
   118→      // Stimulus information
   119→      stimulus_id: trial.stimulusId,
   120→      stimulus_type: trial.stimulusType,
   121→      stimulus_content: trial.stimulusContent,
   122→      stimulus_file: trial.stimulusFile,
   123→      stimulus_onset_time: trial.stimulusOnsetTime,
   124→      stimulus_offset_time: trial.stimulusOffsetTime,
   125→      stimulus_duration: trial.stimulusDuration,
   126→      
   127→      // Response data
   128→      response_key: trial.responseKey,
   129→      response_time: trial.responseTime,
   130→      response_accuracy: trial.responseAccuracy ? 1 : 0,
   131→      response_timeout: trial.responseTimeout ? 1 : 0,
   132→      reaction_time: trial.reactionTime,
   133→      
   134→      // Timing validation
   135→      stimulus_onset_jitter: trial.timingValidation?.onsetJitter,
   136→      frame_drops: trial.timingValidation?.frameDrops,
   137→      refresh_rate: trial.timingValidation?.refreshRate,
   138→      
   139→      // Device information
   140→      input_device: trial.inputDevice,
   141→      device_latency: trial.deviceLatency,
   142→      
   143→      // Experiment metadata
   144→      experiment_name: this.experiment.name,
   145→      experiment_version: this.experiment.version,
   146→      forgescript_version: this.metadata.forgescriptVersion,
   147→      
   148→      // Timestamps
   149→      trial_start_time: trial.trialStartTime,
   150→      trial_end_time: trial.trialEndTime,
   151→      experiment_start_time: this.metadata.experimentStartTime,
   152→      
   153→      // Custom variables (flattened)
   154→      ...this.flattenCustomVariables(trial.customVariables)
   155→    }));
   156→  }
   157→  
   158→  // Generate data dictionary for documentation
   159→  private generateDataDictionary(data: TidyDataRow[]): DataDictionary {
   160→    return {
   161→      participant_id: {
   162→        description: 'Unique identifier for participant',
   163→        type: 'string',
   164→        example: 'P001'
   165→      },
   166→      trial_number: {
   167→        description: 'Sequential trial number within experiment',
   168→        type: 'integer',
   169→        range: [1, data.length]
   170→      },
   171→      condition: {
   172→        description: 'Experimental condition or factor level',
   173→        type: 'categorical',
   174→        levels: [...new Set(data.map(d => d.condition))]
   175→      },
   176→      reaction_time: {
   177→        description: 'Response time in milliseconds from stimulus onset',
   178→        type: 'numeric',
   179→        unit: 'milliseconds',
   180→        missing_value_code: -999
   181→      },
   182→      response_accuracy: {
   183→        description: 'Response accuracy (1 = correct, 0 = incorrect)',
   184→        type: 'binary',
   185→        coding: { 0: 'incorrect', 1: 'correct' }
   186→      },
   187→      stimulus_onset_jitter: {
   188→        description: 'Measured timing precision for stimulus onset',
   189→        type: 'numeric',
   190→        unit: 'milliseconds',
   191→        note: 'Lower values indicate better timing precision'
   192→      }
   193→      // ... additional column descriptions
   194→    };
   195→  }
   196→}

   199→export class BIDSExporter extends AnalysisReadyExporter {
   200→  async exportBIDS(): Promise<BIDSExportResult> {
   201→    const structure = await this.generateBIDSStructure();

   216→

   232→#### ARE-002: R Script Code Generation
   233→**Points: 4 | Priority: High**
   234→
   235→**Description:** Generate analysis-ready R and Python scripts that can immediately load and analyze exported data.
   236→
   237→**Technical Requirements:**
   238→- R script generation with appropriate statistical packages
   239→- Python script generation with pandas and scipy
   240→- Automatic detection of experimental design for correct analysis
   241→- Visualization code for common experimental plots

   243→
   244→**Acceptance Criteria:**

   246→- [ ] Python scripts use pandas best practices for data analysis

   251→
   252→**Script Generation Implementation:**
   253→```typescript
   254→// @psyforge/analysis-generator
   255→export class AnalysisScriptGenerator {
   256→  private experiment: CompiledExperiment;
   257→  private dataSchema: DataSchema;
   258→  private designType: ExperimentalDesign;
   259→  
   260→  constructor(experiment: CompiledExperiment, dataSchema: DataSchema) {
   261→    this.experiment = experiment;
   262→    this.dataSchema = dataSchema;
   263→    this.designType = this.detectExperimentalDesign(experiment);
   264→  }
   265→  
   266→  // Generate R analysis script
   267→  generateRScript(): string {
   268→    const script = new RScriptBuilder()
   269→      .addHeader(this.generateRHeader())
   270→      .addDataLoading(this.generateRDataLoading())
   271→      .addDataPreprocessing(this.generateRPreprocessing())
   272→      .addDescriptiveAnalysis(this.generateRDescriptives())
   273→      .addInferentialAnalysis(this.generateRInferentials())
   274→      .addVisualization(this.generateRPlots())
   275→      .addPowerAnalysis(this.generateRPowerAnalysis())
   276→      .build();
   277→    
   278→    return script;
   279→  }
   280→  
   281→  private generateRHeader(): string {
   282→    return `
   283→# ${this.experiment.name} - Analysis Script
   284→# Generated by Psycho-Forge on ${new Date().toISOString()}
   285→# 
   286→# This script performs standard analyses for the experimental design:
   287→# Design Type: ${this.designType.type}
   288→# Factors: ${this.designType.factors.join(', ')}
   289→# Dependent Variables: ${this.designType.dependentVariables.join(', ')}
   290→
   291→# Load required packages
   292→library(tidyverse)    # Data manipulation and visualization
   293→library(afex)         # ANOVA functions  
   294→library(emmeans)      # Post-hoc comparisons
   295→library(effectsize)   # Effect size calculations
   296→library(performance) # Model diagnostics
   297→library(report)       # Automated reporting
   298→library(pwr)          # Power analysis
   299→
   300→# Set options
   301→options(contrasts = c("contr.sum", "contr.poly"))  # Type III SS
   302→theme_set(theme_minimal())  # Clean ggplot theme
   303→`;
   304→  }
   305→  
   306→  private generateRDataLoading(): string {
   307→    return `
   308→# Load and prepare data
   309→data_file <- "${this.generateFileName('csv')}"
   310→raw_data <- read_csv(data_file, col_types = cols(
   311→  participant_id = col_character(),
   312→  trial_number = col_integer(),
   313→  condition = col_factor(),
   314→  response_accuracy = col_logical(),
   315→  reaction_time = col_double(),
   316→  .default = col_guess()
   317→))
   318→
   319→# Data validation
   320→cat("Data loaded successfully\\n")
   321→cat("Participants:", n_distinct(raw_data$participant_id), "\\n")
   322→cat("Total trials:", nrow(raw_data), "\\n")
   323→cat("Conditions:", paste(levels(raw_data$condition), collapse = ", "), "\\n")
   324→`;
   325→  }
   326→  
   327→  private generateRPreprocessing(): string {
   328→    return `
   329→# Data preprocessing
   330→clean_data <- raw_data %>%
   331→  # Remove practice trials if present
   332→  filter(!str_detect(timeline_id, "practice")) %>%
   333→  
   334→  # Remove trials with missing responses
   335→  filter(!is.na(reaction_time), !is.na(response_accuracy)) %>%
   336→  
   337→  # Remove unrealistic RTs (< 100ms or > 3000ms)
   338→  filter(reaction_time >= 100, reaction_time <= 3000) %>%
   339→  
   340→  # Remove trials with timing errors > 5ms
   341→  filter(abs(stimulus_onset_jitter) <= 5) %>%
   342→  
   343→  # Calculate participant-level exclusions
   344→  group_by(participant_id) %>%
   345→  mutate(
   346→    participant_accuracy = mean(response_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
   347→    participant_mean_rt = mean(reaction_time[response_accuracy], na.rm = TRUE)
   348→  ) %>%
   349→  ungroup() %>%
   350→  
   351→  # Exclude participants with < 60% accuracy
   352→  filter(participant_accuracy >= 0.60) %>%
   353→  
   354→  # Remove outlier RTs (2.5 SD from participant mean)
   355→  group_by(participant_id, condition) %>%
   356→  mutate(
   357→    rt_z = abs(scale(reaction_time)[,1])
   358→  ) %>%
   359→  filter(rt_z <= 2.5) %>%
   360→  ungroup()
   361→
   362→cat("After preprocessing:\\n")
   363→cat("Participants remaining:", n_distinct(clean_data$participant_id), "\\n")
   364→cat("Trials remaining:", nrow(clean_data), "\\n")
   365→cat("Data exclusion rate:", 
   366→    round((1 - nrow(clean_data) / nrow(raw_data)) * 100, 1), "%\\n")
   367→`;
   368→  }
   369→  
   370→  private generateRInferentials(): string {
   371→    if (this.designType.type === 'within_subjects') {
   372→      return this.generateWithinSubjectsANOVA();
   373→    } else if (this.designType.type === 'between_subjects') {
   374→      return this.generateBetweenSubjectsANOVA();
   375→    } else {
   376→      return this.generateMixedDesignANOVA();
   377→    }
   378→  }
   379→  
   380→  private generateWithinSubjectsANOVA(): string {
   381→    return `
   382→# Within-subjects ANOVA
   383→rt_model <- aov_ez(
   384→  id = "participant_id",
   385→  dv = "reaction_time", 
   386→  data = filter(clean_data, response_accuracy == TRUE),
   387→  within = c(${this.designType.factors.map(f => `"${f}"`).join(', ')})
   388→)
   389→
   390→# Model summary
   391→print(rt_model)
   392→print(report(rt_model))
   393→
   394→# Effect sizes
   395→print(eta_squared(rt_model, partial = TRUE))
   396→
   397→# Post-hoc comparisons
   398→rt_emmeans <- emmeans(rt_model, ~ ${this.designType.factors[0]})
   399→print(contrast(rt_emmeans, "pairwise", adjust = "bonferroni"))
   400→
   401→# Accuracy analysis
   402→acc_data <- clean_data %>%
   403→  group_by(participant_id, ${this.designType.factors.join(', ')}) %>%
   404→  summarise(accuracy = mean(response_accuracy), .groups = "drop")
   405→
   406→acc_model <- aov_ez(
   407→  id = "participant_id",
   408→  dv = "accuracy",
   409→  data = acc_data,
   410→  within = c(${this.designType.factors.map(f => `"${f}"`).join(', ')})
   411→)
   412→
   413→print(acc_model)
   414→print(report(acc_model))
   415→`;
   416→  }
   417→}
   418→
   419→// Python script generation
   420→export class PythonAnalysisGenerator {
   421→  generatePythonScript(): string {
   422→    return `
   423→# ${this.experiment.name} - Python Analysis Script
   424→# Generated by Psycho-Forge on ${new Date().toISOString()}
   425→
   426→import pandas as pd
   427→import numpy as np
   428→import scipy.stats as stats
   429→import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
   430→import seaborn as sns
   431→from scipy import stats
   432→import pingouin as pg
   433→
   434→# Load data
   435→df = pd.read_csv('${this.generateFileName('csv')}')
   436→
   437→# Data preprocessing
   438→df_clean = (df
   439→    .query('timeline_id != "practice"')  # Remove practice
   440→    .dropna(subset=['reaction_time', 'response_accuracy'])  # Remove missing
   441→    .query('100 <= reaction_time <= 3000')  # Remove outlier RTs
   442→    .query('abs(stimulus_onset_jitter) <= 5')  # Remove timing errors
   443→)
   444→
   445→# Participant-level filtering
   446→participant_accuracy = df_clean.groupby('participant_id')['response_accuracy'].mean()
   447→valid_participants = participant_accuracy[participant_accuracy >= 0.60].index
   448→df_clean = df_clean[df_clean['participant_id'].isin(valid_participants)]
   449→
   450→print(f"Data loaded: {len(df)} trials, {df['participant_id'].nunique()} participants")
   451→print(f"After cleaning: {len(df_clean)} trials, {df_clean['participant_id'].nunique()} participants")
   452→
   453→# Descriptive statistics
   454→print("\\nDescriptive Statistics:")
   455→desc_stats = df_clean.groupby('condition').agg({
   456→    'reaction_time': ['mean', 'std', 'count'],
   457→    'response_accuracy': 'mean'
   458→}).round(3)
   459→print(desc_stats)
   460→
   461→# Inferential statistics
   462→if len(df_clean['condition'].unique()) == 2:
   463→    # Paired t-test for two conditions
   464→    rt_correct = df_clean[df_clean['response_accuracy'] == True]
   465→    
   466→    condition_1 = rt_correct[rt_correct['condition'] == df_clean['condition'].unique()[0]]['reaction_time']
   467→    condition_2 = rt_correct[rt_correct['condition'] == df_clean['condition'].unique()[1]]['reaction_time']
   468→    
   469→    t_stat, p_val = stats.ttest_rel(condition_1, condition_2)
   470→    effect_size = (condition_1.mean() - condition_2.mean()) / np.sqrt((condition_1.var() + condition_2.var()) / 2)
   471→    
   472→    print(f"\\nPaired t-test: t = {t_stat:.3f}, p = {p_val:.3f}")
   473→    print(f"Cohen's d = {effect_size:.3f}")
   474→    
   475→else:
   476→    # Repeated measures ANOVA for multiple conditions
   477→    rm_anova = pg.rm_anova(data=df_clean[df_clean['response_accuracy'] == True], 
   478→                          dv='reaction_time', within='condition', subject='participant_id')
   479→    print("\\nRepeated Measures ANOVA:")
   480→    print(rm_anova)
   481→
   482→# Visualization
   483→plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))
   484→
   485→plt.subplot(1, 2, 1)
   486→sns.boxplot(data=df_clean[df_clean['response_accuracy'] == True], 
   487→           x='condition', y='reaction_time')
   488→plt.title('Reaction Time by Condition')
   489→plt.ylabel('Reaction Time (ms)')
   490→
   491→plt.subplot(1, 2, 2)
   492→acc_by_condition = df_clean.groupby(['participant_id', 'condition'])['response_accuracy'].mean().reset_index()
   493→sns.barplot(data=acc_by_condition, x='condition', y='response_accuracy', ci=95)
   494→plt.title('Accuracy by Condition')
   495→plt.ylabel('Accuracy')
   496→plt.ylim(0, 1)
   497→
   498→plt.tight_layout()
   499→plt.savefig('analysis_results.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
   500→plt.show()
   501→`;
   502→  }
   503→}
   504→```
   505→
   506→---
   507→
   508→### Epic: Methods Section Generator (MSG)
   509→**Story Points: 6**
   510→
   511→#### MSG-001: Methods Section Generator
   512→**Points: 4 | Priority: High**
   513→
   514→**Description:** Automatically generate publication-ready methods sections from experiment specifications.
   515→
   516→**Technical Requirements:**
   517→- Natural language generation from experiment AST
   518→- APA style formatting and citations
   519→- Technical specification documentation
   520→- Customizable templates for different publication venues
   521→- Integration with common reference management systems
   522→
   523→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   524→- [ ] Generated methods sections follow APA formatting guidelines
   525→- [ ] Technical specifications accurately described from experiment code
   526→- [ ] Participant information and procedures clearly documented
   527→- [ ] Analysis plan appropriately described
   528→- [ ] Output formats support Word, LaTeX, and Markdown
   529→- [ ] Citations automatically formatted and linked
   530→
   531→**Methods Generation Implementation:**
   532→```typescript
   533→// @psyforge/methods-generator
   534→export class MethodsGenerator {
   535→  private experiment: CompiledExperiment;
   536→  private template: MethodsTemplate;
   537→  private citations: CitationManager;
   538→  
   539→  constructor(experiment: CompiledExperiment, template: MethodsTemplate = 'apa') {
   540→    this.experiment = experiment;
   541→    this.template = MethodsTemplates[template];
   542→    this.citations = new CitationManager();
   543→  }
   544→  
   545→  generateMethodsSection(): MethodsSection {
   546→    return {
   547→      participants: this.generateParticipantsSection(),
   548→      apparatus: this.generateApparatusSection(), 
   549→      stimuli: this.generateStimuliSection(),
   550→      procedure: this.generateProcedureSection(),
   551→      design: this.generateDesignSection(),
   552→      dataAnalysis: this.generateDataAnalysisSection(),
   553→      citations: this.citations.getReferences()
   554→    };
   555→  }
   556→  
   557→  private generateParticipantsSection(): string {
   558→    const recruitment = this.experiment.recruitment;
   559→    
   560→    return this.template.format('participants', {
   561→      sampleSize: recruitment?.target?.n || '[N to be determined]',
   562→      ageRange: recruitment?.criteria?.age ? 
   563→        `${recruitment.criteria.age.min}-${recruitment.criteria.age.max} years` : 
   564→        '[age range to be specified]',
   565→      exclusionCriteria: this.formatExclusionCriteria(recruitment?.criteria),
   566→      compensation: recruitment?.compensation ? 
   567→        `$${recruitment.compensation.base} ${recruitment.compensation.currency}` : 
   568→        '[compensation to be specified]',
   569→      recruitmentMethod: this.formatRecruitmentMethod(recruitment?.providers),
   570→      ethicsApproval: '[IRB approval number to be added]'
   571→    });
   572→  }
   573→  
   574→  private generateApparatusSection(): string {
   575→    const devices = this.experiment.devices || [];
   576→    const timing = this.experiment.timingRequirements;
   577→    
   578→    return this.template.format('apparatus', {
   579→      computer: 'Standard laboratory computer with [specifications to be added]',
   580→      monitor: `${timing?.targetRefreshRate || 60}Hz LCD monitor`,
   581→      software: `Psycho-Forge ${this.experiment.forgescriptVersion} ${this.citations.cite('psycho-forge')}`,
   582→      inputDevices: devices.length > 0 ? 
   583→        devices.map(d => d.description).join(', ') : 
   584→        'Standard computer keyboard',
   585→      timingPrecision: timing?.guaranteedPrecision ? 
   586→        `Stimulus timing precision verified to ${timing.guaranteedPrecision}ms` : 
   587→        'High-precision stimulus timing validated',
   588→      additionalEquipment: this.formatAdditionalEquipment(devices)
   589→    });
   590→  }
   591→  
   592→  private generateStimuliSection(): string {
   593→    const stimuli = this.extractStimuliFromExperiment();
   594→    
   595→    const stimuliDescription = stimuli.map(stimulus => {
   596→      switch (stimulus.type) {
   597→        case 'text':
   598→          return `Text stimuli were presented in ${stimulus.fontSize}pt ${stimulus.fontFamily} font, colored ${stimulus.color}`;
   599→        case 'image':
   600→          return `Image stimuli (${stimulus.format}) were ${stimulus.dimensions} pixels`;
   601→        case 'audio':
   602→          return `Audio stimuli were ${stimulus.sampleRate}Hz ${stimulus.format} files`;
   603→        default:
   604→          return `${stimulus.type} stimuli were presented`;
   605→      }
   606→    }).join('. ');
   607→    
   608→    return this.template.format('stimuli', {
   609→      description: stimuliDescription,
   610→      timing: this.describeStimulusTiming(),
   611→      positioning: this.describeStimulusPositioning(),
   612→      counterbalancing: this.describeCounterbalancing()
   613→    });
   614→  }
   615→  
   616→  private generateProcedureSection(): string {
   617→    const timelines = this.experiment.timelines;
   618→    
   619→    const procedureSteps = timelines.map(timeline => {
   620→      const trialCount = timeline.trials?.length || 0;
   621→      const description = timeline.description || `${timeline.id} phase`;
   622→      
   623→      return `${description} consisted of ${trialCount} trials`;
   624→    }).join('. ');
   625→    
   626→    return this.template.format('procedure', {
   627→      overview: procedureSteps,
   628→      trialStructure: this.describeTrialStructure(),
   629→      timing: this.describeOverallTiming(),
   630→      instructions: this.formatInstructions(),
   631→      breaks: this.describeBreaks()
   632→    });
   633→  }
   634→  
   635→  private generateDataAnalysisSection(): string {
   636→    const design = this.experiment.designType;
   637→    const dvs = this.experiment.dependentVariables;
   638→    
   639→    return this.template.format('dataAnalysis', {
   640→      exclusionCriteria: this.formatDataExclusionCriteria(),
   641→      dependentVariables: dvs.join(', '),
   642→      statisticalTest: this.recommendStatisticalTest(design),
   643→      alphaLevel: '0.05',
   644→      corrections: 'Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons',
   645→      software: `R version ${this.citations.cite('r-core')} with packages afex ${this.citations.cite('afex')} and emmeans ${this.citations.cite('emmeans')}`
   646→    });
   647→  }
   648→}
   649→
   650→// Template system for different publication formats
   651→export const MethodsTemplates = {
   652→  apa: {
   653→    format(section: string, data: any): string {
   654→      switch (section) {
   655→        case 'participants':
   656→          return `
   657→**Participants**
   658→
   659→${data.sampleSize} participants (age range: ${data.ageRange}) were recruited via ${data.recruitmentMethod}. Participants were compensated ${data.compensation} for their time. Exclusion criteria included ${data.exclusionCriteria}. The study was approved by the institutional review board (${data.ethicsApproval}).
   660→`;
   661→        
   662→        case 'apparatus':
   663→          return `
   664→**Apparatus**
   665→
   666→The experiment was conducted on a ${data.computer} with a ${data.monitor}. Stimuli were presented using ${data.software}. ${data.timingPrecision}. Responses were collected using ${data.inputDevices}. ${data.additionalEquipment}
   667→`;
   668→        
   669→        case 'procedure':
   670→          return `
   671→**Procedure**
   672→
   673→${data.overview}. ${data.trialStructure} ${data.timing} ${data.instructions} ${data.breaks}
   674→`;
   675→        
   676→        default:
   677→          return `[${section} section to be completed]`;
   678→      }
   679→    }
   680→  },
   681→  
   682→  // Additional templates for other formats
   683→  markdown: { /* Markdown formatting */ },
   684→  latex: { /* LaTeX formatting */ }
   685→};
   686→```
   687→
   688→#### GUI-012: Add Export Menu
   689→**Points: 2 | Priority: Medium**
   690→
   691→**Description:** Integrate data export and methods generation into Studio user interface.
   692→
   693→**Technical Requirements:**
   694→- Export menu with multiple format options
   695→- Progress indicators for large dataset exports
   696→- Preview functionality for methods sections
   697→- One-click export bundles (data + scripts + methods)
   698→- Export history and re-export capabilities
   699→
   700→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   701→- [ ] Export menu accessible from main Studio interface
   702→- [ ] Multiple export formats available with clear descriptions
   703→- [ ] Progress indicators show export status for large datasets
   704→- [ ] Methods section preview allows editing before export
   705→- [ ] Bundle export includes all necessary files for analysis
   706→- [ ] Export history allows re-downloading previous exports
   707→
   708→---
   709→
   710→### Epic: Documentation & Best Practices
   711→**Story Points: 3**
   712→
   713→#### DOC-003: Write "Literate Design" Guide
   714→**Points: 3 | Priority: Medium**
   715→
   716→**Description:** Create comprehensive documentation for reproducible experimental design using Psycho-Forge.
   717→
   718→**Technical Requirements:**
   719→- Best practices guide for experiment design
   720→- Code style guidelines for ForgeScript
   721→- Reproducibility checklist and validation
   722→- Example workflows and case studies
   723→- Integration with academic publishing requirements
   724→
   725→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   726→- [ ] Complete guide covers experiment design workflow
   727→- [ ] Code examples demonstrate best practices
   728→- [ ] Reproducibility checklist ensures experiment validity
   729→- [ ] Case studies show real-world applications
   730→- [ ] Documentation integrated into Hub and Studio help systems
   731→
   732→---
   733→
   734→## Definition of Done
   735→
   736→**For each ticket:**
   737→- [ ] Implementation with comprehensive unit tests (>90% coverage)
   738→- [ ] Export functionality tested with real experimental data
   739→- [ ] Generated scripts validated with statistical software
   740→- [ ] Methods sections reviewed for accuracy and completeness
   741→- [ ] **Gemini Pro audit passed**: User experience, academic accuracy, export quality
   742→- [ ] **O3 audit passed**: Data export integrity, script generation, statistical correctness
   743→
   744→**For sprint completion:**

   746→- [ ] Generated R and Python scripts successfully analyze exported data
   747→- [ ] Methods sections automatically generated with publication quality
   748→- [ ] Complete workflow from design to publication demonstrated
   749→- [ ] Documentation supports reproducible research practices
   750→
   751→---
   752→
   753→## Quality Gates & Audit Requirements
   754→
   755→### Full-Cycle Workflow Testing
   756→- [ ] End-to-end testing from experiment design to data analysis
   757→- [ ] Generated scripts tested with real experimental datasets
   758→- [ ] Methods sections reviewed by academic researchers
   759→- [ ] Export formats validated with external tools (BIDS validator, R, Python)
   760→- [ ] Publication workflow tested with journal submission requirements
   761→
   762→---
   763→
   764→## Risk Mitigation
   765→
   766→**Academic Risks:**

# Revolutionary Component System Integration Strategy

## Executive Summary

After analyzing the existing psyforge sprint structure, I've identified a strategic approach to integrate the revolutionary component system while maintaining project discipline and momentum. The current project is in a **pre-Sprint 1 state** with excellent foundations already established, making this the optimal time for architectural evolution.

## Current Project State Analysis

### ✅ **Strengths to Leverage**
- **Solid Foundation**: All 4 core packages (timing, scheduling, devices, forgescript) implemented and building
- **Quality Gates**: Mandatory dual-AI audit system (Gemini Pro + O3) already established
- **Complete Sprint Design**: All 10 sprints comprehensively planned with clear deliverables
- **Development Infrastructure**: Nx monorepo, CI/CD pipeline, and quality standards operational

### 🎯 **Strategic Integration Opportunity**
The timing is **perfect** for component system integration because:
- Sprint 1 hasn't started yet (no disruption to active development)
- Foundation packages are complete but not yet integrated into GUI
- Sprint 3 (GUI View Layer) is where component system provides maximum value
- Sprint 4 (Live Mirror-Canvas) will benefit enormously from component architecture

## Integration Strategy: The "Component Revolution" Approach

### Phase 1: Foundation Enhancement (Pre-Sprint 1)
**Timeline**: 1 week
**Impact**: Enhances existing foundation without disrupting sprint schedule

#### 1.1 Component Architecture Integration
- **Enhance existing `@psyforge/forgescript` package** with component primitives
- **Maintain backward compatibility** with existing fluent API
- **Add new component-based experiment definition** as alternative approach
- **Integrate with existing timing/scheduling/devices packages**

#### 1.2 Update Sprint Planning
- **Sprint 3 modification**: Replace basic GUI with component-driven architecture
- **Sprint 4 enhancement**: CRDT synchronization becomes component-aware
- **Sprint 5 amplification**: AI assistant can now suggest component compositions

### Phase 2: GUI Revolution (Sprint 3 Integration)
**Timeline**: During planned Sprint 3 (no schedule disruption)
**Impact**: Transforms GUI from basic timeline view to revolutionary component system

#### 2.1 Component-Driven Visual Design
- **Replace planned timeline viewer** with component canvas
- **Implement drag-and-drop component assembly**
- **Create component library browser** alongside Monaco editor
- **Enable visual component composition** with live preview

#### 2.2 Enhanced Developer Experience
- **Component IntelliSense** in Monaco editor
- **Visual component debugging** with time-travel simulator
- **Component performance profiling** in RAPD framework

### Phase 3: Ecosystem Amplification (Sprint 4-7)
**Timeline**: During planned sprints (enhances existing objectives)
**Impact**: Multiplies the value of existing planned features

#### 3.1 Collaborative Component Development
- **CRDT-synchronized component editing** (Sprint 4)
- **AI-assisted component generation** (Sprint 5)
- **Component marketplace** in Hub (Sprint 7)

#### 3.2 Universal Component System
- **Device-aware components** integrate with DAL
- **Paradigm library as component collections**
- **Export system understands component structure**

## Specific Sprint Modifications

### Sprint 1: No Changes Required ✅
- **Timing precision**: Still critical foundation
- **Hierarchical scheduler**: Now supports component execution
- **Device abstraction**: Enhanced to work with component system

### Sprint 2: Minor Enhancement 📈
- **ForgeScript compiler**: Extended to handle component definitions
- **Component validation**: Added to existing validation pipeline
- **Stroop task**: Can now be built with components OR fluent API

### Sprint 3: Revolutionary Transformation 🚀
- **Replace**: Basic visual timeline viewer
- **With**: Component-driven visual design system
- **Add**: Component library browser
- **Enhance**: Monaco editor with component IntelliSense

### Sprint 4: Amplified Collaboration 🔄
- **CRDT synchronization**: Now component-aware
- **Visual-code binding**: Handles component composition
- **Real-time collaboration**: Component-level granularity

### Sprint 5-10: Ecosystem Multiplication 🌟
- **AI assistant**: Suggests component compositions
- **Hub**: Becomes component marketplace
- **Analysis export**: Component-aware data structures
- **Alpha launch**: With revolutionary component system

## Risk Mitigation Strategy

### 1. Backward Compatibility
- **Maintain fluent API**: Existing sprint examples continue working
- **Gradual migration**: Teams can adopt components incrementally
- **Dual approach**: Support both fluent API and component system

### 2. Quality Assurance
- **Existing audit gates**: Apply to component system development
- **Component validation**: Integrated into existing quality pipeline
- **Performance monitoring**: RAPD framework tracks component performance

### 3. Timeline Protection
- **No sprint delays**: Component system enhances rather than replaces
- **Parallel development**: Component system develops alongside existing features
- **Incremental rollout**: Component features added progressively

## Implementation Timeline

### Week 1: Foundation Integration
- Extend `@psyforge/forgescript` with component primitives
- Update existing packages to support component execution
- Modify Sprint 3 planning to include component system

### Week 2-3: Sprint 1 Execution (Unchanged)
- Continue with planned timing precision implementation
- Ensure component system integrates with precision timing
- Validate component execution performance

### Week 4-5: Sprint 2 + Component Enhancement
- Implement component-aware compiler
- Build component validation system
- Create example components for classic paradigms

### Week 6-7: Sprint 3 - GUI Revolution
- Implement component-driven visual design
- Create component library browser
- Integrate with Monaco editor

### Week 8+: Continue Enhanced Sprint Schedule
- Each subsequent sprint gains component system benefits
- Ecosystem features multiply in value
- Alpha launch includes revolutionary component system

## Success Metrics

### Technical Success
- **Component system performance**: Matches or exceeds fluent API
- **Developer experience**: Faster experiment creation with components
- **Integration quality**: Seamless operation with existing systems

### Strategic Success
- **Maintains sprint discipline**: No schedule disruption
- **Accelerates development**: Component reuse increases team velocity
- **Enhances market position**: Revolutionary differentiation while maintaining quality

### User Success
- **Backward compatibility**: Existing users unaffected
- **Enhanced productivity**: Component system provides genuine value
- **Ecosystem growth**: Component marketplace drives adoption

## Conclusion

This integration strategy transforms psyforge from an excellent platform into a **revolutionary component-driven system** while maintaining the disciplined development approach that makes this project exceptional. The timing is perfect, the integration is strategic, and the benefits are exponential.

The component system doesn't disrupt the sprint schedule - it **amplifies every planned feature** and creates a platform that will truly end the reproducibility crisis through precision engineering AND unprecedented developer productivity.

**Ready to proceed with the Component Revolution?** 🚀

After analyzing the existing psyforge sprint structure, I've identified a strategic approach to integrate the revolutionary component system while maintaining project discipline and momentum.     35→
    36→/**
    37→ * Create a new scheduler instance with default configuration
    38→ * optimized for psychological experiments
    39→ */
    40→export function createScheduler(options: SchedulerOptions = {}): Scheduler {
    41→  return new Scheduler({
    42→    maxTasksPerFrame: 10,
    43→    frameTimeBudget: 8, // 8ms for 120fps headroom
    44→    useHighPrecisionTiming: true,
    45→    timingTolerance: 2, // 2ms tolerance for ≤2ms target
    46→    allowPreemption: true,
    47→    ...options
    48→  });
    49→}
    50→
    51→/**
    52→ * Create a new timeline with experiment-friendly defaults
    53→ */
    54→export function createTimeline(name: string, options: Omit<TimelineOptions, 'name'> = {}): Timeline {
    55→  return new Timeline({
    56→    name,
    57→    defaultPriority: TaskPriority.NORMAL,
    58→    repeatable: false,
    59→    repetitions: 1,
    60→    ...options
    61→  });
    62→}
    63→
    64→/**
    65→ * Create a repeating timeline for trial-based experiments
    66→ */
    67→export function createTrialTimeline(
    68→  name: string, 
    69→  repetitions: number, 
    70→  repetitionDelay: number = 0,
    71→  options: Omit<TimelineOptions, 'name' | 'repeatable' | 'repetitions' | 'repetitionDelay'> = {}
    72→): Timeline {
    73→  return new Timeline({
    74→    name,
    75→    repeatable: true,
    76→    repetitions,
    77→    repetitionDelay,
    78→    defaultPriority: TaskPriority.NORMAL,
    79→    ...options
    80→  });
    81→}
    82→
    83→/**

    86→export function createTask(
    87→  name: string,
    88→  execute: TaskFunction,
    89→  priority: TaskPriority = TaskPriority.NORMAL,
    90→  options: Omit<TaskOptions, 'priority'> = {}
    91→): Task {
    92→  return new Task(name, execute, { priority, ...options });
    93→}
    94→
    95→/**
    96→ * Helper to create a stimulus presentation task
    97→ */
    98→export function createStimulusTask(
    99→  name: string,
   100→  stimulusFunction: TaskFunction,
